data.title: Rigetti gets initiated with bullish views at Craig-Hallum
data.description:
Quantum computing stocks have been receiving a lot of attention on Wall Street, with the latest action being from Benchmark, which raised D-Wave Quantum's (NYSE:QBTS) price target, and Craig-Hallum, which began coverage of Rigetti Computing (NASDAQ:RGTI) with a Buy rating.

Shares of D-Wave fell about 6% on Friday, while Rigetti was down about 2%.

D-Wave Quantum: Benchmark's Analyst David Williams said they were increasing the price target to $8 from $3 following their meeting with D-Wave's CFO John Markovich. The sentiment for quantum has clearly shifted over the last few weeks, with multiple industry and company-specific announcements helping drive the price inflection, he added.

The analyst thinks a recent capital raise eliminates the risk discount driven by much liquidity and dilution concerns, which they viewed as the largest overhang to the stock. However, with both concerns now cleared and the raise considerably less dilutive than potentially expected, the analyst believes the D-Wave story has become significantly more attractive.

Williams said he remains encouraged by the strength of the company's new programs in the pipeline and expect the cadence of deployments to accelerate as the new go-to-market strategy ramps up.

The analyst noted that D-Wave's go-to-market has historically been through its quantum-as-a-service platform and Professional Services business but did not have a hardware sales component. However, the company has become increasingly open to moving in this direction, which could generate high single digit millions in revenue per unit.

On the government front, the recent "Awardable" designation by the Department of Defense, or DOD, Tradewinds should help accelerate procurement and broaden access to D-Wave's platform, supporting quantum annealing adoption across the military complex, according to the analyst.

Tradewinds is DOD's platform for sourcing, funding, and developing solutions to challenges in the artificial intelligence, machine learning, digital, and data analytics space.

Williams believes that the company is positioned to benefit from the National Quantum Initiative Reauthorization Act over the next five years and sees room for upside if the firm gains traction in hardware sales.

Rigetti Computing: Craig-Hallum initiated coverage of the Berkeley, Calif.-based company with a Buy rating and $12 price target.

Analysts led by Richard Shannon said quantum computing has an opportunity to revolutionize the world like very few technologies can, and though its commercial impact is still years in the future, its market size and disruptive potential for classical compute makes it relevant to investors today. Rigetti has unique scaling advantages which may allow it to achieve equivalency or superiority versus its direct peers, and to achieve quantum advantage in the next few years.

The analysts added that Rigetti and other quantum computing names, in the recent weeks, have been caught up in a retail-driven fervor.

Although this environment creates risks for investors in the near-term that the analysts do not downplay, but they provided other perspectives, such as — the transformative impact of Al was not recognized by the markets until all at once in the spring of 2023, and investors seem to be expecting the next elemental step in computing.

In addition, the analysts said that even after this run up, the $1B to $8B valued quantum computing companies are still small compared to the more than $1T market cap classical compute-driven companies that could be disrupted.

The analysts also said that there is scarcity value in the public markets for quantum computing plays. As such, they think Rigetti and quantum computing deserve a place in investors' portfolios today.

Shannon and his team noted that classical computing is a huge market, measured in trillions of dollars, and quantum computing can displace some of it, while serving new problems classical compute cannot, which shows that quantum computing addresses enormous markets.

The analysts added that because of its potential to displace this large classical computing market, the potential for mergers and acquisitions by trillion dollar market cap companies that are threatened is real. As the most mature U.S.-based pure play superconducting quantum computing company, Rigetti is uniquely positioned to benefit from this type of interest, the analysts noted.

Shares of quantum computing related stocks have surged this year. Year-to-date, Quantum Computing (QUBT) has skyrocketed over 1,500%, Rigetti Computing (RGTI) has soared over 650%, and D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) has gained more than 600%. IonQ has added more than 200%, while Arqit Quantum (ARQQ) has jumped over 125%.

data.title: Lowell Farms appoints Philippe Faraut as CFO
data.description:
• Lowell Farms (OTCQB:LOWLF) on Friday announced the appointment of Philippe Faraut as chief financial officer (CFO), effective immediately.
  • Most recently, Faraut served as CFO of iAnthus. Prior to that, Faraut was CFO of Irwin Naturals.
  • LOWLF is +9.92% to $0.0155.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow pare losses as core PCE comes softer than expected
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages pared some losses on Friday, after the November's core PCE report came in cooler than expected.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.5%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.9% and the Dow (DJI) was -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 5 basis points to 4.51%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 5 basis points to 4.26%.

The Federal Reserve's favored inflation gauge - core PCE - ticked down in November, and edged up 0.1% M/M in November, less than the +0.2% consensus.

A stopgap spending plan backed by President-elect Donald Trump was rejected in the House of Representatives, further fueling concerns of a government shutdown.

"The big question now is whether a U.S. government shutdown is about to happen," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The latest bill was voted down by 235 votes to 174. The funding is set to expire on Friday night.

"A short-lived shutdown affects government workers, but has limited economic impact. The longer a shutdown lasts, the more disruptive it is to the U.S. economy. Economic data may not get published in a shutdown," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Wall Street's bounce back on Thursday following its worst session in over three months ran out of steam in the final hour of trading.

"For those wanting a quiet run up to Xmas, the good news is that there hasn't been any real follow-through to the Fed-induced slump on Wednesday. The bad news is that an initial recovery in markets struggled to gain traction yesterday," Reid added.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Affirm raised to Market Outperform as JMP calls it a 'long-term secular winner'
data.description:
JMP analyst David Scharf upgraded Affirm Holdings (NASDAQ:AFRM) shares to Market Outperform from Market Perform on Friday, calling it a "long-term secular winner at the expense primarily of the credit card industry."

Further gains are expected from continued adoption of buy now, pay later solutions for everyday payments, he wrote in a note, adding the company "benefits from a more robust loan platform relative to competitors."

Shares are already up nearly 40% since Affirm's (NASDAQ:AFRM) Q1 2025 earnings released on Nov. 7, though Scharf remains "constructive on the shares at current levels," based on prospects for a friendlier regulatory environment, as well as a faster-than-expected outlook for a return to GAAP profitability, better unit economics and user engagement, and momentum in new alliances and geographies."

JMP's Market Outperform rate diverges from the SA Quant system rating and the average SA analyst rating, both at Hold, but aligns with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

(AFRM) shares edged up 0.4% minutes after the opening bell.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: SBUX, CCL and more
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Friday pared earlier losses after key inflation data came in soft. Here are some stocks to watch on Friday:

  • Shares of FedEx (FDX) surged around 8% in pre-market trading, after the parcel delivery giant said it intends to separate its FedEx Freight division into a new publicly listed company, in a move that was anticipated by many. “This is the right time to pursue a separation as we respond to the unique dynamics of the LTL (Less-Than-Truckload) market,” top boss Raj Subramaniam said. Analysts responded favorably to the announcement. The global economic bellwether separately reported a FQ2 2025 top-line miss and lowered its annual revenue guidance.
  • Starbucks (SBUX) stock slipped nearly 1% ahead of the opening bell. The decline came amid a scheduled strike by unionized baristas in Los Angeles, Chicago, and Seattle. Members of Starbucks Workers United earlier this week voted to authorize the strike "to win fair raises, benefits and staffing, protest unfair labor practices, and resolve outstanding litigation with Starbucks (SBUX)." The world's largest coffee chain, for its part, has asked the union to return to the bargaining table.
  • Shares of Winnebago Industries (WGO) slipped about 2% ahead of market open, after the Eden Prairie, Minn.-based company reported a FQ1 2025 top- and bottom-line miss. Winnebago (WGO), which makes recreational vehicles (RVs), camper vans, and trailers, swung to a quarterly loss from a profit, and its revenue slumped 18% Y/Y. Chief executive Michael Happe said the RV and marine operating environment had "remained challenging" in the quarter, "marked by subdued consumer demand and a cautious dealer network."
  • Carnival (CCL) stock advanced nearly 5% in pre-market trading, after the world's largest cruise line operator delivered a top- and bottom-line FQ4 2024 beat. "Even with less inventory available, booking volumes taken during the fourth quarter of 2024 for 2025 were higher than the prior year for a strong 2024, despite the traditionally slower period around the election," Carnival (CCL) said. Additionally, the company forecasted adjusted net income of about $2.3B for full year 2025, representing growth of more than 20% than 2024.

More on the markets

  • 2025 Markets To Be Driven By Liquidity, Lobbying And Collectibility - Most Likely
  • Canadian Banks Are Seeing Increased Risks
  • Stagflation Fears And Soaring U.S. Yields: S&P 500's Next Move
  • Jay Powell's tenure as Fed chair has seen weakest market performance on decision days
  • Wall Street rebound fizzles out, but Dow snaps longest losing streak since 1974

data.title: SEC charges Entergy with internal accounting controls violations
data.description:
• The Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday announced that Entergy Corporation (NYSE:ETR) agreed to pay a $12 million civil penalty to settle charges that it failed to maintain internal accounting controls.
  • According to the SEC’s complaint, Entergy included materials and supplies at their average cost as an asset on its balance sheets from at least mid-2018 to the present.
  • However, Entergy had allegedly been informed by its employees and management consultants that this asset included a substantial amount of potential surplus.
  • According to the complaint, Entergy failed to establish a comprehensive process to review these materials and supplies to identify surplus, remeasure it, and record any differences between its average cost and remeasured cost as an expense, in accordance with GAAP.
  • Without admitting or denying the allegations in the SEC's complaint, Entergy consented to the entry of a final judgment, subject to court approval, including paying the $12 million civil penalty and adopting an independent consultant’s recommended improvements to its internal accounting controls.
  • ETR -0.59% premarket to $74.05.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Onfolio names Adam Trainor as interim CFO
data.description:
• Onfolio (NASDAQ:ONFO) said it has appointed Adam Trainor as its interim finance chief, effective January 1.
  • The firm said Trainor will also continue in his current role as chief operations officer.
  • Trainor has been with the company since October 2020.

data.title: Occidental Petroleum rises after Berkshire Hathaway raises stake
data.description:
• Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) was rising Friday pre-market after an SEC filing by Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) said the latter has increased its stake in the energy company.
  • OXY shares were trading +2.95% at $46.70.
  • Berkshire Hathaway bought an additional 8.9M shares of common stock in multiple transactions at prices ranging from $45.80 to $46.56.

data.title: Battalion Oil Corporation terminates merger agreement with Fury Resources
data.description:
• Battalion Oil Corporation (NYSE:BATL) on Friday said it has terminated the previously disclosed merger agreement with Fury Resources.
  • Fury was not able to meet the obligations and close the transaction contemplated by the merger agreement, the company said.
  • BATL -3.49% premarket to $2.35.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pitney Bowes repays additional $75M of Oaktree Capital senior secured notes
data.description:
• Pitney Bowes (NYSE:PBI) said on Friday that it is repaying an additional $75 million of the $275 million in senior secured notes issued by Oaktree Capital Management.
  • Prior to the repayment, the balance of the Oaktree Notes was $172.25 million.
  • This repayment follows the repayment of $100 million previously announced on November 19, 2024, and is part of the company’s previously announced strategic initiative to deleverage its balance sheet.
  • The repayment was funded by cash on hand. The company anticipates having additional updates in the near-term on its plans to retire the remainder of the Oaktree 2028 Notes.
